# Anti-Patterns — What the Artist Must Reject

**Anti-Pattern 1: Seeking Approval. External validation corrupts authenticity.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Rushing. Pushing for results kills creativity.**

**Case: The Critics. Ignore critics. They do not know your path.**

**Case: The Competition. Comparing yourself to others is a distraction.**

**Case: The Production. Focusing on output over life is a mistake.**

**Case: The Fear. Fear of not being good enough paralyzes.**
---

**Case: The Loud World. The noise of the world drowns out the inner voice.**
**Case: The Busyness. Keeping busy avoids the deeper questions.**
**Case: The Romance. Seeking love as completion is a misunderstanding.**
**Case: The Certainty. Pretending to have answers you do not have.**
**Case: The Impatience. Wanting results now destroys the process.**
**Case: The Fashion. Following artistic trends is a betrayal of your own voice.**
**Case: The Fame. Seeking fame corrupts the artist.**
**Case: The Comparison. Comparing your journey to others is a trap.**
**Case: The Despair. Despair is part of the creative process. Do not resist it.**
**Case: The Excuses. Do not make excuses for not creating.**
**Case: The Performance. Creating for an audience instead of yourself corrupts the work.**
